article.title: Uber | L4 | OA
article.content:
I recently appeared for the online assessment for L4. I dont remember everything clearly but here is a gist of what was asked.

Q1 was easy. I dont remember but it was something very simple like finding prefix sum and returning the index when it crossed a certain value. 

Q2. Had 2 vectors called animation and soundtracks. Animation vector had string entries that looked something like this ["<animation1, 10>", "<animation2, 20>", "<animation3, 40>"] where each entry was a string of animation name followed by the length of the animation in time. Similarly soundtrack had string entries that looked something like this ["<track1, 6>", "<track2, 4>", "<track3, 5>"]. 
The aim was to find which soundtrack can be used for which animation given the condition that the track time should be a multiple of the animation time. In case of multiple answers, select the lowest index one. 
(i am not sure about this but the output was also a vector of strings where each entry looked like <trackselected, no of times the track should be played>)
output: ["<track3, 2>", "<track2, 5>", "<track3,10>"]

Q3. Given a matrix comprising of three characters : 
   '#' -> obstacle
   'F' -> connected element
   '-' -> empty space 

   There is only one connected component of Fs and it moves together.
   How will the output matrix look if we were allowed to move the connected element via gravity? 

if input is 
            F F #
            - F -
            # F -
            - - -
            - - -
            - # -

Then output is 
               - - #
               F F -
               # F -
               - F -
               - - -
               - # -
               

It's like the connected component came down to as much as possible because of gravity. So it stays at this level because there is an obstacle at [2,1].

Q4. We will get a vector of segments [[1,1], [2,8], [4,7], [16,19]] where [1,1] means we are covering elemt 1 to 1 (both inclusive), So [2,8] means we are covering elements 2 to 8 and so on. We need to return a vector containing how many elements were covered after every segment cumulatively. Sometimes segments may be overlapping but not completely. 
Output is [1, 8, 8, 12]
After segment 1, element 1 was covered
After segment 2, 2-8 were covered along with previous 1 so 8
After segment 3, 4-7 were covered but no change as it was already covered so 8
After segment 4, 16-19 were covered so 4 + previous 8 so 12

post.content: For Q4 are the segments sorted?
